commands.review_summary:
The PR removes the duplicated core OpenAI Codex OAuth implementation, delegates legacy login and refresh helpers through provider-owned OpenAI plugin auth hooks, and adds optional provider auth context hooks for cancellation and manual-code input.

PR surface: Source -660, Tests -470. Total -1130 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a refactor PR, not a reported runtime bug. Source inspection shows current main still has duplicated OAuth implementation while the branch delegates the legacy wrappers to the provider hook.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human auth/API compatibility review is the next action because the PR is a maintainer-owned draft with protected labels and changes ProviderAuthContext plus deprecated OpenAI OAuth wrappers.
commands.reason: comment version already processed in ledger

commands.author: RomneyDa

target.title: refactor(openai): centralize Codex OAuth flow
target.body:
## Summary

- Refactor OpenAI Codex OAuth to remove duplicated browser/token flow code; no user-visible OAuth behavior change is intended.
- Keep the existing OpenAI plugin OAuth runtime as the single implementation used by both plugin auth and legacy compatibility wrappers.
- Preserve legacy `src/llm` login/refresh behavior, including abort propagation, immediate manual-code input, callback handling, and credential refresh shape.
- Provenance: duplicated OpenAI Codex OAuth flow was introduced by `bb46b79d3c14` (`refactor: internalize OpenClaw agent runtime`, #85341).


## Compatibility Review

- Deprecated `src/llm` OpenAI Codex OAuth login/refresh entry points are preserved and covered by focused compatibility tests. The login wrapper still reports browser auth through `onAuth`, still routes fallback prompts through `onPrompt`, still starts `onManualCodeInput` in parallel with browser auth when supplied, and now passes the caller `signal` into the provider-owned OAuth hook.
- The plugin-facing `ProviderAuthContext` additions are optional-only (`signal?`, `onManualCodeInput?`) and existing provider auth hooks do not need to supply or consume them. The OpenAI Codex provider forwards them only to the OpenAI plugin OAuth runtime; ordinary plugin callers continue to use the plugin-owned manual fallback handler.
- Current-main token behavior remains plugin-owned: bounded token request timeout, SSRF-guarded fetch, cancellation, TLS/proxy preflight, callback handling, and error rewriting stay in `extensions/openai/openai-codex-oauth*.runtime.ts` instead of being copied back into core.
- Live OpenAI browser OAuth with real credentials was not run and is not required for this refactor before maintainer review: this PR removes duplicate implementation and delegates to the existing plugin runtime, while focused tests/build/lint/autoreview cover the changed compatibility and delegation surfaces. A live credential run can still be requested separately if maintainers want release-style auth proof.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/openai/openai-codex-oauth-flow.runtime.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/openai/openai-codex-provider.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/openai/provider-auth.contract.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/provider-openai-codex-oauth.test.ts --reporter=verbose --testTimeout=5000`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/llm/utils/oauth/openai-codex.test.ts --reporter=verbose --testTimeout=5000`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth.openai-codex-refresh-fallback.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/llm/utils/oauth/openai-codex.ts src/llm/utils/oauth/openai-codex.test.ts src/plugins/provider-openai-codex-oauth.ts src/plugins/provider-openai-codex-oauth.test.ts src/plugins/types.ts extensions/openai/openai-codex-oauth.runtime.ts extensions/openai/openai-codex-provider.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm build`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenAI Codex OAuth implementation duplication between the bundled OpenAI plugin and legacy core OAuth helpers; no user-visible OAuth behavior change is intended.
Real environment tested: Local macOS worktree with installed repo dependencies.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: The commands listed in Verification were run after the final code changes.
Evidence after fix: Focused OpenAI Codex OAuth, provider auth, compatibility, refresh fallback tests passed; focused oxlint and `git diff --check` passed; `pnpm build` passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.
Observed result after fix: Legacy compatibility surfaces now delegate to the existing provider-owned OpenAI Codex OAuth implementation while preserving cancellation and parallel manual-code input behavior, and the duplicated core browser/token implementation is removed.
What was not tested: Live OpenAI browser OAuth against real OpenAI credentials was not run.
